title: Former Stoughton cop accused of killing Sandra Birchmore denied bail pending trial
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Former Stoughton officer Matthew Farwell denied bail pending trial for allegedly murdering Sandra Birchmore in 2021.
Judge M. Page Kelley cited strong evidence and potential flight risk as reasons for Farwell's continued detention.
Farwell accused of strangling Birchmore, 23, and staging her death as a suicide; trial set for October 5.

title: Joe Mazzulla named NBA Coach of the Year for 2025-26 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Joe Mazzulla wins the NBA Coach of the Year for 2025-26, the first Celtics coach honored since 1980.
The Celtics finished with a 56-26 record, overcoming low expectations and a major roster overhaul.
Mazzulla credited the coaching staff and players for the award, downplaying his individual achievement.

title: New Hampshire mother and son found dead in suspected murder-suicide
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dover police found Eileen Shevelin, 78, and her son Thomas Wallace, 57, dead at their home in New Hampshire.
Authorities suspect the incident to be a murder-suicide, with Wallace suffering a gunshot wound to the head.
Autopsies are scheduled in Concord, N.H., as the investigation continues with no threat to the public identified.

title: Red Sox get good news on Roman Anthony, Garrett Crochet’s injuries
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony progresses from hand injury, feels better after batting practice with increased swings planned.
Garrett Crochet improves from shoulder inflammation, throws live batting practice at Fenway Park without issue.
Red Sox manager Chad Tracy optimistic on Anthony and Crochet as both show recovery in recent sessions.

title: Mass. police respond after ‘body’ reported in truck, turns out to be a mannequin
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A 911 call in Wakefield reported a potential dead body in a truck, prompting police response.
Wakefield police discovered that the reported body was actually a mannequin used for fire and rescue training.
The incident was clarified on Facebook, confirming no dead body was found in the truck in Wakefield.

title: Man spent hours between wall and elevator shaft at Fields Corner MBTA station, police say
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A man was trapped for hours between a wall and an elevator shaft at Fields Corner MBTA station.
The man, claiming to be homeless, illegally entered the Dorchester station seeking a place to sleep.
Firefighters rescued him at 3:30 p.m.; he faces a trespassing charge, according to Transit Police.

title: 2-year-old bitten by raccoon at Mass. town beach
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 2-year-old was bitten by a raccoon at Wayland Town Beach and taken to Newton-Wellesley Hospital.
Authorities issued a safety warning, advising against feeding or approaching wild animals and emphasizing medical care after bites.
Wayland Police are searching the area for the raccoon and will monitor the beach in the coming days.

title: 3 Boston cheese shops perfect for the ultimate summer picnic spread
author: Kelly Egan
bullets: American Provisions in South Boston offers artisanal cheeses, charcuterie boards, and wines, located near M Street beach.
Bacco's Wine and Cheese in Back Bay provides international cheeses and grab-and-go picnic boxes near Boston Public Garden.
Crane River Cheese Club in Beacon Hill focuses on New England cheeses, offering beer-infused cheddars and Cheese Club Kits.

title: Boston to scale back Open Newbury Street events this year, Wu says
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Boston will reduce Open Newbury Street events this year due to a packed calendar of major city events.
Mayor Wu announced a reduction in car-free weekends on Newbury Street amid World Cup and city anniversary events.
The city has not yet released the new dates for Open Newbury Street, typically closing traffic for 10 Sundays annually.

title: Millionaires tax surges past expectations, once again
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts' millionaires tax generated over $3.1 billion this fiscal year, exceeding the initial $2 billion estimate.
Despite predicted affluent resident exodus, over 150,000 taxpayers left in 2023, causing a net loss of $4.18 billion in income.
Lawmakers face decisions on budget use of tax revenue amid economic uncertainties and potential federal funding cuts.
